summary: Iteration 29 (refund-loophole reversal on reschedule, end_date shift, attendance off-day flow, counsellor attendance visibility) validated. 32/32 backend pytest PASSED in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration29_refund_reversal_attendance.py covering: module imports (no syntax/compile errors), source-level guarantees for all 4 features, HTTP contracts (auth + role gating) for /api/attendance/mark, /api/teacher/reschedule-class, /api/counsellor/student-attendance/{student_id}, plus admin can fetch counsellor/student-attendance and response excludes Mongo _id. Frontend smoke: admin login at /login redirected to /admin-dashboard with 'Welcome back' toast and all admin APIs returned 200. UI source verified: TeacherDashboard.js handles needs_reason (lines 122-127), reason dialog with reason-forgot/reason-rescheduled testids and class picker (lines 844-880), attendance table shows off-day + reason badges (lines 900-911). CounsellorStudents.js fetches /counsellor/student-attendance/{user_id} (line 43) and renders off-day-marked records with amber styling (lines 230-245). Teacher/Student E2E flows still not exercisable (OTP-gated, passwords unknown).
critical_code_review_comments: attendance.py:50-64 needs_reason path returns ONLY {needs_reason, available_classes} when reason missing (no 4xx). The UI handles this correctly today, but it overloads HTTP 200 for what is effectively a precondition gate — any non-UI client must inspect body.needs_reason. Document in API spec or move to 422 with structured body.

attendance.py:42-47 has_class_today query allows status='completed' classes. If a class already completed on a prior date, marking attendance for today might still match because date<=today<=end_date. Consider also checking that 'today' falls within session days or excluding completed assignments to avoid spurious matches.

teacher.py:745 end_date = new_date + (duration_days-1 + reschedule_num). On the FIRST reschedule, reschedule_num=1, so end_date is shifted by 1 day beyond duration — matches the spec, but cumulative reschedules add reschedule_count days each time (1→3→6...), which compounds. Verify product intent is incremental shift PER reschedule, not cumulative every call.

teacher.py:763-782 refund_reversal looks up an assignment in 'refunded' state and reverses ONLY ONE such assignment per reschedule. If multiple refunded assignments exist between the teacher and student (rare but possible), only the first is reverted. Consider explicit ordering (sort by refunded_at desc) and a comment.

counsellor.py:80-88 endpoint returns ALL attendance records for the given student_id without scoping by counsellor or by the counsellor's assigned students. Any authenticated counsellor can read any student's attendance. If multi-tenant isolation matters, scope to records whose teacher_id is in the counsellor's tracked teachers, or by assigned_students relation.
attendance.py 'reason' is free-form (no enum). Spec says only 'forgot_to_mark' or 'rescheduled_class'. Add validation: if reason and reason not in allowed_set: 400.

attendance.py:78-83 record only sets class_id if non-null but spec says off-day attendance MUST link to a class. Backend should enforce class_id required when off_day_marking=True (currently permitted because the upper class_id check only fires when reason is missing path; reason+null class_id+ no class_today still raises 400, OK — but if has_class_today is True yet client sends class_id, you accept and store it — ambiguous semantics).

success_rate.frontend: Admin login + dashboard verified end-to-end (200 on /api/auth/login + redirect to /admin-dashboard + welcome toast + 13 admin APIs returning 200). Teacher/counsellor/student UI flows verified via source grep only — no OTP-gated credentials available.

test_credentials: Admin info@kaimeralearning.com / solidarity&peace2023 — confirmed working. Teacher/counsellor/student passwords still unknown (OTP-gated). Auth uses 'session_token' (not 'token'); cookie-based session.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing seeded admin and live DB for read-only HTTP contract checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration29_refund_reversal_attendance.py (32 tests). Auth note: login response is {session_token, user}; use cookie session_token=<value> and/or Authorization: Bearer <value>. To exercise SUCCESS path of refund reversal: (1) login as a teacher with an approved+paid assignment, (2) cancel a class via /api/teacher/cancel-class/{id}, (3) verify student credits incremented and a 'class_cancellation_refund' transaction exists, (4) call /api/teacher/reschedule-class/{id} with new schedule, (5) verify (a) student credits decremented by credits_required, (b) 'reschedule_recharge' transaction with negative amount exists, (c) end_date == new_date + duration_days - 1 + reschedule_count days, (d) class status flipped back to 'scheduled'. To exercise full refund reversal, need an assignment with payment_status='refunded' that gets flipped to 'paid' with a 'refund_reversal' transaction. Off-day attendance: as teacher, POST /api/attendance/mark for a date with no class — expect 200 + needs_reason=true + available_classes; resend with reason='forgot_to_mark' and class_id — expect 200 with off_day_marking, reason, class_id persisted in attendance collection.
